mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which signals near-term earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, with Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 320 indicate entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of overlapping observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output that immediately undermines earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines greater than 0.5 percent are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of historical instances.
